name: Lires - Muxía - vuelta de Costa del Morte
region_intro: Mountain biking around Cee offers diverse terrain along the rugged Costa da Morte in Galicia, Spain. The region features a wild coastline with steep cliffs and sandy shores, transitioning to verdant inland areas. Mountain bike routes traverse a mix of Atlantic ocean views, forests, and rural landscapes. The varied elevation provides options for different skill levels. Best mountain bike trails around Cee The most popular mountain bike trail is Lires - Muxía - vuelta de Costa del Morte, a difficult 36.9 miles (59.4 km) trail that takes 5 hours 28 minutes to complete. This route offers significant elevation gain and expansive coastal views. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Talón Beach – Cape Finisterre Lighthouse loop from Fisterra, a moderate 13.8 miles (22.2 km) path. This trail provides scenic views of the lighthouse and the Atlantic coastline. Local mountain bikers also love the Cape Finisterre Lighthouse – Talón Beach loop from Estorde, a 13.4 miles (21.6 km) trail leading through coastal areas and past the iconic lighthouse, often completed in about 1 hour 23 minutes. Mountain biking around Cee is defined by dramatic coastal scenery, verdant inland areas, and diverse terrain. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy coastal paths to more challenging routes with significant climbs. The routes in Cee are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 27 reviews. More than 260 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Cee's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Cee?
A: Cee offers a significant network of mountain bike routes, with over 20 recorded trails in the area. These routes cater to various skill levels, from easy coastal paths to more challenging ascents and descents.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Cee?
A: Mountain bike trails around Cee feature diverse terrain along the rugged Costa da Morte. You'll encounter a mix of wild coastline with steep cliffs, sandy shores, and verdant inland areas. The routes often provide Atlantic ocean views, forests, and rural landscapes, with varied elevation offering options for different skill levels.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Cee?
A: Yes, Cee has options for beginners. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/3990044" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cape Finisterre Lighthouse – Talón Beach loop from Estorde&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 13.4-mile (21.6 km) trail that leads through coastal areas and past the iconic lighthouse.

Q: What are some challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: Experienced riders looking for a challenge can explore routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e982853780/lires-muxia-vuelta-de-costa-del-morte" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lires - Muxía - vuelta de Costa del Morte&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 36.9-mile (59.4 km) trail with significant elevation gain and expansive coastal views. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38147278" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of Our Lady of the Boat – A Ferida Monument loop from Caberta&lt;/a&gt;, which covers nearly 60 km with substantial climbs.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes around Cee?
A: Yes, many routes around Cee are designed as loops. Popular circular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10501481" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Talón Beach – Cape Finisterre Lighthouse loop from Fisterra&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 13.8-mile (22.2 km) path, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/3990044" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cape Finisterre Lighthouse – Talón Beach loop from Estorde&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What interesting landmarks or attractions can I see along the mountain bike trails?
A: The trails around Cee offer access to several notable landmarks. You can ride past the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/218305" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fisterra Lighthouse&lt;/a&gt;, a traditionally significant point considered the 'end of the world.' Other points of interest include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/261550" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Kilometre Zero Marker at Cape Finisterre&lt;/a&gt; and various scenic beaches like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/237558" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mar de Fóra Beach&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any waterfalls accessible from mountain bike routes in Cee?
A: Yes, the region around Cee is home to beautiful waterfalls. The famous &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4803815" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ézaro Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, where the Xallas River plunges directly into the Atlantic, is a significant natural attraction. You might also find smaller cascades like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4585105" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fervenza da Mexadoira&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6042321" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Caldeiras do Castro Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about the trails in Cee?
A: The mountain bike trails in Cee are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.1 stars from over 27 reviews. Riders often praise the dramatic coastal scenery, the diverse terrain that caters to different abilities, and the stunning views of the Atlantic coastline and Fisterra Lighthouse.

Q: Is Cee a good destination for family-friendly mountain biking?
A: Cee's diverse network includes easier coastal paths that can be suitable for families. While specific family-designated trails aren't highlighted, the presence of moderate and easy routes, combined with scenic beaches like Gures and Estorde for relaxation, offers opportunities for enjoyable outings. Always check individual route details for suitability.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Cee?
A: The Costa da Morte region, where Cee is located, experiences an Atlantic climate. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures and fewer crowds, making them ideal for mountain biking. Summers can be warm, while winters are milder but may bring more rain. Checking local weather forecasts before your ride is always recommended.

Q: Are there options for parking or public transport to access trailheads in Cee?
A: Cee is a town with infrastructure, and parking is generally available within the town itself. For specific trailheads, it's advisable to check the starting points of individual routes on komoot for parking information. As a strategic stop on the Camino Finisterre Muxía, Cee also has some public transport connections, though direct access to all trailheads might require a short ride or walk.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the mountain bike trails in Cee?
A: Many outdoor areas in Galicia are dog-friendly, especially on natural trails. However, specific regulations can vary. It's generally recommended to keep dogs on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or other users, and to clean up after them. Always check for local signage or specific rules for each trail or protected area you plan to visit.
tips: The Lires estuary is the smallest of the Galician estuaries, so much so that many people do not know it. Located in the heart of the Costa da Morte, between the capes of Touriñán and Finisterre, the Castro River opens to the sea after passing through the small town of Lires. At low tide you can cross to the other side, where Nemiña beach opens out to the sea, very popular with surfers.

The sanctuary is baroque in style with influences from classicism. The first written document that remains of the temple is from the year 1544. The chapel was rebuilt several times, until the current one was built at the beginning of the 18th century, in 1719, thanks to the donation of the Counts of Maceda, whose ashes are found in some tombs inside the sanctuary.

Gravel paths that lead to the viewpoint of this mountain, more than three hundred meters above sea level, from where you have a panoramic view of the ocean. Be careful when riding in winter as the wind can be very strong

waypoints.name: Sanctuary of Our Lady of the Boat
